mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding economic output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and forward 20-day returns below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or sign flips to negative in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5%, it signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and depress equity valuations, leading to negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE > 5.0, the subsequent 20-day return is positive more than 50% of the time or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below trailing 12-month CPI inflation, real interest rates are negative, encouraging borrowing and risk-taking that boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When real rates are negative, the 20-day forward return is negative more than 45% of time or the average return is not significantly different from zero at 5% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: In the early-cycle recovery phase of the economy, rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand that translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: During periods classified as early-cycle recovery, the correlation between RSAFS changes and forward 20-day returns is negative or IC below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator for residential investment and broader economic momentum; an increase in starts predicts higher GDP growth 1-2 quarters ahead, which lifts equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between HOUST monthly change and forward 60-day returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year period, or the signal generates negative returns in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and declines in the unemployment rate confirm that economic growth is sustained and broad-based, creating a virtuous cycle that boosts corporate earnings and equity prices over 20 days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously, the forward 20-day return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the joint signal does not improve upon individual signals.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 1% signals that the Fed is actively tightening policy to combat inflation, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative one-week forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 1.0, the average 5-day forward return is positive or not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 3% erodes real purchasing power and prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, which reduce equity valuations and produce negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods with CPI > 3% yoy, the 20-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average excess return over low-inflation periods is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
